other_observers: Peter Gent, Chris Nunes; Found a week or two earlier by observers unknown to me
description: Large white owl on ground in field. Back, median (and lesser?) wing coverts moderately checkered in black. Greater coverts and tertials lightly marked with black. Primaries white. nape, hindneck, crown appeared mostly white, but upper and outer parts of facial disks lightly marked, setting off white facial disks. Sides white. Remainder of underparts not seen. Eyes yellow.Behaviors: Sitting quietly on ground, awake, occasionally swiveling headCall: not heardPlumage: probably adult
